title: Reusable jar sterilising powder
subtitle: A dry sodium percarbonate powder for sterilising glass jars, bottles, and tools used in homemade cosmetics
excerpt: A batch of sodium percarbonate powder kept in a sealed jar gives an on-demand sterilising solution for glass jars, dropper bottles, and mixing tools. Add 2 tablespoons to warm water, soak for 15 minutes, rinse thoroughly. The powder keeps for 6 to 12 months. One batch makes many sterilising solutions. No bleach, no strong fumes.
sourceNotes: Oxygen-based sterilising solution for cosmetic and home use. Sodium percarbonate (2Na2CO3·3H2O2): releases hydrogen peroxide on contact with water; effective against bacteria, yeast, and mould spores. Use at approximately 5 to 8 g per litre of warm water. Rinse-off application; no residue after thorough rinsing. Safe for glass, stainless steel, and most plastics. Not recommended for aluminium.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally for a larger batch. The powder itself is sodium percarbonate; the blending step is optional but ensures even distribution of any added optional ingredients.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix and store in a sealed glass jar. Ready immediately.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 to 9 months in a sealed container away from moisture. Once sodium percarbonate is damp it begins to degrade. Keep the lid on between uses.

glossaryTerms.definition: A dry compound that releases hydrogen peroxide when dissolved in water. Effective against bacteria, yeast, and mould. Sold as an oxygen bleach or eco-laundry booster. Decomposes to water, oxygen, and sodium carbonate, leaving no harmful residue after rinsing.

Sterilising eliminates all microorganisms, including spores; requires heat or pressure as well as chemical action. Sanitising reduces microbial load to a safe level. This powder sanitises effectively for cosmetic-making purposes without the equipment needed for full sterilisation.

content.text: releases oxygen as it dissolves, breaking down organic matter and microbial contamination on contact surfaces. It covers the

distinction well for cosmetic-making jars and tools: the jar needs to be clean and sanitised, not clinically sterile. Keeping a jar of this powder in the workroom means a fresh sanitising soak is 2 tablespoons away. No bleach smell, no harsh residue after rinsing.

items.prepNote: Sold as oxygen bleach, eco-laundry booster, or percarbonate of soda; store dry

content.text: Weigh the sodium percarbonate powder directly into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid. Label with the name and date. That is the entire preparation step; no mixing with other dry ingredients is needed.

Add 2 tablespoons (approximately 14 g) to 1 litre of warm water (40 to 50°C; hot water works faster but the reaction is more vigorous). Stir to dissolve. Submerge the glass jars, bottles, and tools to be sanitised. Leave for 15 minutes. Rinse thoroughly with clean water. Allow to air dry or dry with a clean cloth. Do not use on aluminium items.
6 to 9 months in a sealed container away from moisture. Damp sodium percarbonate begins to slowly release its oxygen and loses effectiveness. Keep the lid on tightly between uses.
